2609da06-6bd7-4c4e-a4fa-e40dc2fe3fa2.title:
Find the current population of San Francisco as reported by three different authoritative sources (government, news outlet, and research institution). For each source, provide: the population figure, the date of the data, and a direct citation URL. Then identify any discrepancies between the sources and explain which figure is most likely accurate and why.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:
Find the current population of San Francisco as reported by three different authoritative sources (government, news outlet, and research institution). For each source, provide: the population figure, the date of the data, and a direct citation URL. Then identify any discrepancies between the sources and explain which figure is most likely accurate and why.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

f0546d4c-e460-4a94-9a21-dba098af4373.title: Search for and gather San Francisco population figures from a government source, a major news outlet, and a research institution (then visit top results to extract exact figures, dates, and URLs)

details.intent: Find authoritative, recent population estimates for San Francisco and gather exact population number, the date (year or estimate date), and a direct citation URL for each of three source types: (1) government, (2) news outlet, (3) research institution. Prepare to visit the best matching URLs to extract precise text.
details.plan: 1) Run targeted web searches to locate the most authoritative and recent pages for each source type. 2) Visit the top government page (US Census QuickFacts or California Dept. of Finance or SF City official page) to extract the population figure and estimate date. 3) Visit a major news outlet's article (e.g., San Francisco Chronicle, NYTimes, Washington Post) that reports a recent SF population number and extract figure, date, and URL. 4) Visit a reputable research institution's page/report (e.g., Public Policy Institute of California, Brookings, Pew Research, or academic report) that provides a recent SF population estimate and extract figure, date, and URL. 5) Record each source_name, population_value (explicit numeric), date_of_estimate (as reported), direct_URL (citation), and any methodological note found. 6) Compare figures, note discrepancies (numeric differences and differing dates/methods), and explain which figure is most likely accurate and why (preferring most authoritative and recent methodology).
target_facts_to_extract: short_method_note (e.g., census estimate, DOF estimate, news report citing source)
likely_sources_to_search_and_visit: U.S. Census Bureau — QuickFacts San Francisco city, California
California Department of Finance — E-1 Population Estimates for Cities and Counties
City and County of San Francisco official population page or SF Planning population estimates
San Francisco Chronicle article reporting recent population estimates
New York Times / Washington Post article if they have SF population report
Public Policy Institute of California (PPIC) or Brookings / Pew Research report on SF population or metro trends
example_search_queries: San Francisco population California Department of Finance 2023 E-1 population estimates
San Francisco population City and County of San Francisco 2023 population
San Francisco population 2023 article San Francisco Chronicle population
Public Policy Institute of California San Francisco population 2023
details.follow_up_actions_after_search: Visit the top 1-2 URLs for each source type to extract exact numbers and dates. If a source cites another official estimate (e.g., news citing Census or DOF), visit the cited official page to confirm.

comparison_and_conclusion.discrepancies: string summary of numeric/date differences and likely causes
comparison_and_conclusion.most_likely_accurate_figure: which source/figure and why (methodology, recency, authority)

details.why_this_step_reduces_uncertainty: Searching first identifies authoritative pages and recent reporting; visiting those pages extracts precise, citable figures and dates. Collecting one government, one news, and one research-institution source gives diverse perspectives and lets us compare methodology, recency, and citation chains so we can judge which figure is most reliable.
details.next: Run the specified searches and then visit the top government, news, and research-institution URLs to extract the required fields.

Plan reconciliation approach: define 'current population', comparability (city vs metro), and discrepancy analysis framework

Define what counts as 'San Francisco population' (City and County boundary vs SF metro area), prioritize sources likely to be comparable (Census/official estimate year), and outline how to explain discrepancies (different vintages, ACS vs estimates, pandemic-era revisions, city vs metro). Specify final extraction fields: population figure, reference date/year, geography definition, and citation URL.

results.title: Demographics of San Francisco - Wikipedia
results.description: The 2022 Census Bureau American Community Survey put the population at 808,437: a decrease of 65,528 from 2020. With a population density of 18,633 per square mile (7,194/km2), San Francisco is the second-most densely populated major American city, behind only New York (among cities greater ...

results.title: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts: San Francisco County, California
results.description: For more information, please visit the 2024 5-year ACS Comparison Guidance page. Fact Notes ... -Either no or too few sample observations were available to compute an estimate, or a ratio of medians cannot be calculated because one or both of the median estimates falls in the lowest or upper interval of an open ended distribution. NData for this geographic area cannot be displayed because the number of sample cases is too small. QuickFacts data are derived from: Population Estimates, American Community Survey, Census of Population and Housing, Current Population Survey, Small Area Health Insurance Estimates, Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates, State and County Housing Unit Estimates, County Business Patterns, Nonemployer Statistics, Economic Census, Survey of Business Owners, Building Permits.
